title: sclerocactus glaucus subsp. wetlandicus
intro: Sclerocactus glaucus subsp. wetlandicus, also known as the Wetland Sclerocactus, is a rare and endangered species of cactus native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the wetland areas of New Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Sclerocactus glaucus subsp. wetlandicus include its potential to soothe burns and wounds, as well as its ability to alleviate pain and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and powdering the stems, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams for medicinal use.
